Risk factors are correlated / binned among each other
which do you care about more : "Prevalent" lables
 - are they interested in continuous predictors or only categorical ones.

does follow up end when ANY event is encountered?



Some variables are of more interest 
Age, diabetes, blood pressure (systolic).
Several whether or not to include
coronary heart disease, blood pressure meds (Y/N), smoking status, Total cholesterol, BMI.
 - baseline values for all.
 
 Don't want people who have already had a stroke.
 
 Don't need a competing risk model
 any other events can be treated as censoring.
 
 time varying should just be for diabeties and blood pressure
 
 365.25 to account for leap year.
 
 covariates can differ in final stratified model.
 
 survivial piece only interested in first period
 
 Introduction: This section describes your understanding of the project and puts into words the (statistical) hypotheses you will be testing/evaluating in the final report (1-2 paragraphs).
Preliminary methods section: This section describes your data analysis plan, including any data cleaning, as you currently plan to implement. This section can change as you conduct the analysis. (<1 page)
The data analysis plan should be no more than 1.5 pages double spaced 12 point arial font or similar.


Risk profiles
high blood prsessure,

someone who has diabetes

someone who ahs bP and diabetes

anothers risk profeils we find interesting.

for age 40,50, and 60

What would analysis look like for time-varying?
